title: Tempest Signs New VALORANT Roster for 2025 Season
excerpt: We're excited to announce the addition of Jett to our VALORANT roster, bringing fresh talent and aggressive playstyle to the team.
content:
Tempest eSports is thrilled to announce the signing of Kim 'Jett' Min-jun to our VALORANT roster ahead of the 2025 competitive season. The 19-year-old South Korean prodigy brings an electrifying playstyle that perfectly complements our existing lineup.

Jett previously competed in VCT Challengers Korea, where he consistently ranked among the top performers in the region. His aggressive entry fragging and clutch potential caught the attention of our coaching staff during the off-season scouting period.

'We've been looking for a player who can add another dimension to our attack,' said Head Coach Alex Thompson. 'Jett brings exactly the kind of fearless, high-impact play that we need to compete at the highest level.'

The addition of Jett rounds out our five-player roster alongside Phoenix, Sage, Sova, and Omen. The team will begin bootcamp preparations immediately ahead of the VCT 2025 season opener.
author: Tempest Management

title: Championship Victory: CS2 Team Takes Home Major Trophy
excerpt: Our CS2 team showed incredible resilience and tactical brilliance, securing the championship at the ESL Pro League Season 18.
content:
In a thrilling grand final that went the distance, Tempest CS2 emerged victorious at the ESL Pro League Season 18, defeating Astralis 3-2 in a best-of-five series.

The road to the championship was anything but easy. After dropping to the lower bracket in the group stage, our team rallied with five consecutive victories, including decisive wins over NAVI and FaZe Clan.

In-Game Leader Pierre 'IGL' Laurent delivered a masterclass in tactical Counter-Strike, consistently outmaneuvering opposing teams with innovative strategies. AWPer Nikola 'AWPer' Petrovic was named tournament MVP after recording the highest average damage per round in the playoffs.

'This trophy means everything to us,' said IGL after the final. 'We went through so many setbacks to get here. This team never gives up.'

The victory marks Tempest's first major CS2 championship and cements the organization's position as a serious contender in the competitive Counter-Strike scene.
author: Sarah Chen

title: Behind the Scenes: Training Camp Documentary
excerpt: Go behind the scenes of our intensive pre-season training camp where our teams prepare for the challenges ahead.
content:
Our latest documentary takes you inside the Tempest training facility for an exclusive look at how our professional players prepare for the competitive season.

The three-week bootcamp brought together players from all three rosters for intensive practice sessions, team-building exercises, and strategic planning with coaching staff.

Highlights from the documentary include:

- Morning routine and physical fitness regimen
- VOD review sessions with head coaches
- Custom scrimmage schedules against top-tier opponents
- Sports psychology workshops
- Team bonding activities

The documentary is now available on our YouTube channel and gives fans unprecedented access to life as a professional esports athlete.

'We wanted to show the dedication and sacrifice that goes into competing at this level,' said Marketing Director Emma Johnson. 'These players put in incredible work that fans rarely see.'
author: Content Team

title: New Partnership with Gaming Peripheral Giant HyperX
excerpt: Tempest eSports announces a major partnership with HyperX, providing cutting-edge gaming peripherals for all our teams.
content:
We are proud to announce a strategic multi-year partnership with HyperX, one of the world's leading gaming peripheral brands.

As part of this partnership, HyperX will provide all Tempest teams with their premium line of gaming headsets, keyboards, and mice. The collaboration also includes co-branded merchandise and exclusive content featuring our players.

'HyperX shares our commitment to performance excellence,' said CEO Michael Chen. 'Their products are trusted by the best players in the world, and we're excited to have them supporting our teams.'

HyperX representatives will also be present at major tournaments where Tempest competes, setting up dedicated fan zones and product demonstration areas.

The partnership represents one of the largest sponsorship deals in Tempest history and underscores the growing interest from major brands in the esports ecosystem.
author: Business Development

title: Quarter-Finals Finish at IEM Katowice
excerpt: Our CS2 team showed incredible resilience reaching the quarter-finals at IEM Katowice 2024, setting the stage for future success.
content:
Tempest CS2 delivered a strong performance at IEM Katowice 2024, advancing through the group stage and securing a quarter-finals finish against some of the best teams in the world.

The team's run included impressive victories over established organizations and demonstrated significant tactical growth under Head Coach Henrik Larsson.

While the quarter-final elimination was disappointing, the team showed they belong among the elite. Key performances from Entry Fragger Max 'Entry' Mueller and Support player Tomasz 'Support' Kowalski were standout moments of the tournament.

'IEM Katowice was a learning experience,' said Coach Larsson. 'We identified areas for improvement and the team is already working on refining our approach for the next major event.'
author: Sarah Chen

title: League of Legends Team Reaches ERL Spring Split Finals
excerpt: Our LoL roster delivers an outstanding season, reaching the finals of the European Regional League Spring Split.
content:
Tempest League of Legends has capped off an incredible Spring Split by reaching the ERL finals, their best result since joining the league.

Led by Mid Laner Giovanni 'MidLaner' Rossi, who was named MVP of the regular season, the team went on a dominant 14-4 run through the split. Their aggressive early-game style proved difficult for opponents to counter.

The playoffs saw the team overcome two best-of-five series before falling in a closely contested grand final. Despite the runner-up finish, Head Coach Carlos Mendez expressed optimism about the team's trajectory.

'We've built something special here,' said Mendez. 'The team chemistry is incredible and we're only going to get better. Summer Split is our time.'
author: Content Team
